title: Chuck Hagel on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Chuck Hagel, 24th U.S. Secretary of Defense, former U.S. Senator from Nebraska, and former Belfer Center Visiting Fellow, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about his advice to the next Secretary of State, bringing Bowe Bergdahl home, being a pioneer in the cellphone industry, and how he likes to insert a bit of fun into a serious job.
body: Chuck Hagel was the 24th Secretary of Defense, serving from February 2013 to February 2015. He is the only Vietnam veteran and the first enlisted combat veteran to serve as Secretary of Defense. Secretary Hagel also served two terms in the United States Senate (1997-2009) representing the state of Nebraska. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features an interview with Chuck Hagel, the 24th U.S. Secretary of Defense (2013-2015), on the 'Office Hours' podcast. It highlights his unique background as the only Vietnam veteran and first enlisted combat veteran to hold the Secretary of Defense position. The piece also notes his prior service as a two-term U.S. Senator from Nebraska. The content is primarily biographical, detailing his career milestones within the context of national security and defense policy discussions. The broader page includes links to related content on topics such as NATO's Article 5, emerging technology in defense, and nuclear proliferation risks, situating Hagel's insights within ongoing international security dialogues.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了美国第24任国防部长查克·哈格尔（2013-2015年在任）接受“Office Hours”播客采访的相关内容。文章强调了他独特的背景：他是唯一一位曾参加越南战争、也是首位以士兵战斗经历出身担任国防部长的官员。同时提及他曾担任内布拉斯加州两届美国参议员的从政经历。内容主要为人物传记性质，概述了其在美国国家安全和国防政策讨论背景下的职业生涯重要节点。页面还包含指向相关内容的链接，涉及北约第五条、国防新兴技术、核扩散风险等主题，将哈格尔的观点置于持续进行的国际安全对话框架之中。
